items.title: Preserve metadata
items.avoid: Returning a bare wrapper function that loses the original name and docstring.
items.prefer: Always wrap the inner function with @functools.wraps(fn).
items.why: Introspection, logging and documentation tools rely on accurate __name__ and __doc__.
items.example:
```python
@functools.wraps(fn)
def wrapper(*a, **kw): ...
# fn.__name__, fn.__doc__, fn.__signature__ all preserved
```

items.title: Keep decorators thin
items.avoid: Putting heavy business logic inside the wrapper.
items.prefer: Limit decorators to one cross-cutting concern (timing, retry, auth) and delegate real work to the wrapped function.
items.why: Thin decorators stay reusable and easy to reason about when stacked.
